metadata.note: All data verified via web search Feb 2026. Hours and availability may change — always call ahead to confirm.

resources.name: Siena Francis House
resources.description: Largest provider of emergency shelter to adults experiencing homelessness in Nebraska. Provides emergency shelter, meals, substance abuse treatment, and supportive services.
resources.eligibility: Adults experiencing homelessness. No ID required for emergency shelter.

resources.name: Open Door Mission
resources.description: Provides 917 safe shelter beds daily for men, women, and families. Serves over 5,000 meals daily. Offers long-term recovery programs and outreach centers.
resources.hours: Emergency shelter open 24 hours. Office: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM
resources.eligibility: Men, women, and families in need. Call 402-422-1111 to seek emergency shelter.

resources.name: Stephen Center - Pettigrew Emergency Shelter
resources.description: Only shelter in the Omaha area that keeps families together. Partners with individuals, families, and the community to overcome homelessness, substance abuse, and mental health concerns.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day. Intake: 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM
resources.eligibility: Single men and women age 19+, and families (all family definitions). Must be sober upon admission.

resources.name: People's City Mission
resources.description: Lincoln's primary emergency shelter since 1907. Provides emergency and transitional housing to men, women, children, and families including single mothers.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
resources.eligibility: Men, women, children, and families in need of emergency or transitional housing.

resources.name: Matt Talbot Kitchen & Outreach
resources.description: Lincoln's only community kitchen and day center for individuals and families experiencing homelessness and poverty. Serves nutritious meals twice daily.
resources.hours: Mon, Tue, Fri: 9 AM - 3 PM; Wed: 11 AM - 3 PM; Thu: 9 AM - 1 PM. Lunch: 11:30 AM - 12:30 PM; Dinner: 5:30 PM - 6:30 PM daily.
resources.eligibility: Open to anyone in need. No requirements.

resources.name: Crossroads Mission Avenue - Grand Island
resources.description: 90-bed facility providing emergency shelter and a four-phase program to help homeless men, women, and families get back on their feet.
resources.eligibility: Men, women, and families experiencing homelessness.

resources.name: Crossroads Mission Avenue - Hastings
resources.description: Emergency homeless shelter and food pantry providing meals, shelter, and case management in the Hastings area.
resources.eligibility: Men, women, and families experiencing homelessness.

resources.name: Hope Harbor
resources.description: Emergency shelter providing housing assistance for individuals and families in the Grand Island area.
resources.eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness.

resources.name: The Connection Homeless Shelter
resources.description: Emergency shelter for single men, single women, and families in North Platte. Provides meals, case management, and supportive services.
resources.hours: Open 24/7. Breakfast at 6:30 AM, Supper at 6:30 PM.
resources.eligibility: Single men, single women, and families. $7/night suggested but not required.

resources.name: CAPWN Supportive Housing Programs
resources.description: Community Action Partnership of Western Nebraska provides supportive housing programs and homeless prevention services in the Panhandle region.
resources.eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness in the Panhandle region.

resources.name: Panhandle Youth Shelter (CAPWN)
resources.description: Licensed 14-bed youth shelter serving youth in the Panhandle of Nebraska since 1984. Open 24/7 for youth experiencing homelessness.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
resources.eligibility: Youth experiencing homelessness in the Panhandle region.

resources.name: Food Bank for the Heartland
resources.description: Major food bank providing emergency and supplemental food to neighbors across Nebraska and western Iowa. Partners with over 600 agencies.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM. Call SNAP/Food Resources Hotline: 1-855-444-5556
resources.eligibility: Distributes through partner agencies. Visit foodbankheartland.org/food-resources/find-food/ to locate nearest pantry.

resources.name: Community Action Mid-Nebraska Food Bank
resources.description: Community Action food bank and food rescue program serving central Nebraska with mobile produce pantries and emergency food assistance.
resources.eligibility: Income-qualified individuals and families.

resources.name: Northeast Community Action Partnership - Food Pantries
resources.description: Operates food pantries in Norfolk, Creighton, Pender, and South Sioux City serving northeast Nebraska.
resources.hours: Varies by location. Call for specific pantry hours.
resources.eligibility: Income-qualified individuals and families in northeast Nebraska.

resources.name: Eastern Nebraska Community Action Partnership - Bellevue Food Pantry
resources.description: Consumer choice food pantry serving the Bellevue community with groceries and emergency food assistance.
resources.eligibility: Income-qualified individuals and families in eastern Nebraska.

resources.name: Central Nebraska Community Action Partnership
resources.description: Provides food pantries, rent and utility assistance, case management, WIC, Head Start, immunization clinics, and other emergency assistance.
resources.eligibility: Income-qualified individuals and families in central Nebraska.

resources.name: Women's Center for Advancement (WCA)
resources.description: Safe place for anyone experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, or human trafficking. Provides free, high-quality services addressing immediate safety needs and long-term independence.
resources.eligibility: Anyone in the Omaha area experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, or human trafficking.

resources.name: Voices of Hope
resources.description: Provides services to victims and their significant others facing sexual assault, domestic violence, stalking, human trafficking, incest, and childhood abuse.
resources.hours: 24-hour crisis line. Office: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
resources.eligibility: Victims and significant others affected by sexual assault, domestic violence, stalking, human trafficking, or childhood abuse in the Lincoln area.

resources.name: Friendship Home
resources.description: Lincoln's domestic violence shelter providing confidential emergency shelter, crisis intervention, and comprehensive support services.
resources.eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and their children. Deaf/Hard of Hearing: 402-261-0545.

resources.name: Hope Crisis Center
resources.description: Fighting domestic violence in southeast and central Nebraska. Serves Fillmore, Gage, Jefferson, Saline, Seward, Thayer, and York Counties.
resources.address: Offices in Fairbury, Beatrice, York, Seward, and Crete
resources.eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence in Fillmore, Gage, Jefferson, Saline, Seward, Thayer, and York Counties.

resources.name: DOVES Program
resources.description: Domestic violence program serving the Nebraska Panhandle. Provides crisis intervention, temporary shelter in hotel rooms, and advocacy services.
resources.eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence in the Nebraska Panhandle region.

resources.name: Bright Horizons
resources.description: Domestic violence program serving northeast Nebraska. Provides crisis intervention, shelter, and support services.
resources.eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence in northeast Nebraska.

resources.name: S.A.F.E. Center
resources.description: Domestic violence and sexual assault crisis center serving the Kearney and central Nebraska area.
resources.eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault in central Nebraska.

resources.name: Nebraska Coalition to End Sexual and Domestic Violence
resources.description: Statewide advocacy organization with 20 member programs providing direct services to survivors across Nebraska. Can help connect to local DV programs.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM. For 24/7 help call local member programs.
resources.eligibility: Anyone seeking domestic violence or sexual assault services in Nebraska.

resources.name: Legal Aid of Nebraska - Omaha Office
resources.description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income Nebraskans. Handles housing, family law, consumer, benefits, and other civil matters.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM. AccessLine: call to apply for services.
resources.eligibility: Low-income individuals. Douglas County: 402-348-1060. Outside Douglas County: 1-877-250-2016. Elder AccessLine (60+): 1-800-527-7249. Native American: 1-800-729-9908. Spanish: 1-877-669-8898.

resources.name: Legal Aid of Nebraska - Grand Island Self-Help Center
resources.description: Self-help legal center in the Hall County Courthouse providing assistance with legal forms, information, and referrals.
resources.address: 111 West First Street (Hall County Courthouse, lower level)
resources.eligibility: Low-income individuals. Apply via AccessLine: 1-877-250-2016.

resources.name: Nebraska Volunteer Lawyers Project
resources.description: Provides free legal assistance through volunteer attorneys for civil legal matters. Operates walk-in clinics at courthouses.
resources.address: Douglas County Courthouse, 17th & Farnam Street (Omaha); Lancaster County Courthouse, 575 S 10th Street (Lincoln)
resources.hours: Omaha: Mon & Wed 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM. Lincoln: Thu 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM.
resources.eligibility: Low-income individuals with civil legal matters. Does not handle immigration cases.

resources.name: Center for Legal Immigration Assistance (CLIA)
resources.description: Lincoln-based nonprofit offering comprehensive, pro bono, and affordable legal immigration services.
resources.eligibility: Immigrants needing legal assistance. Offices in Omaha, Columbus, Grand Island, Lexington, and Council Bluffs.

resources.name: Nebraska Immigration Legal Assistance Hotline
resources.description: Statewide hotline connecting immigrants with legal services and information about their rights.
resources.eligibility: Immigrants in Nebraska seeking legal assistance or information.

resources.name: OneWorld Community Health Centers - South Omaha
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center providing comprehensive medical, dental, and behavioral health care with sliding scale fees.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee scale based on income. Accepts Medicaid, Medicare, and most insurance. Uninsured welcome.

resources.name: OneWorld Community Health Centers - Bellevue
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center satellite clinic serving the Bellevue community with comprehensive medical services.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee scale based on income.

resources.name: OneWorld Community Health Centers - Northwest
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center satellite clinic serving northwest Omaha.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee scale based on income.

resources.name: OneWorld Community Health Centers - Plattsmouth
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center satellite clinic serving the Plattsmouth community.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee scale based on income.

resources.name: Charles Drew Health Center
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center providing comprehensive medical, dental, and behavioral health services to the North Omaha community.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee scale based on income. Accepts Medicaid, Medicare, and most insurance.

resources.name: Bluestem Health
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center operating five integrated family medicine clinics in Lincoln. Serves over 21,000 patients annually with sliding fee discount.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri. Call for specific clinic hours.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee discount for underinsured and uninsured. Accepts most insurance.

resources.name: Bluestem Health - 27th Street Clinic
resources.description: Bluestem Health satellite clinic location in Lincoln.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee discount available.

resources.name: CAPWN Community Action Health Center
resources.description: Community health center in the Panhandle region providing medical, dental, and behavioral health services with supportive health services.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Sliding fee scale available.

resources.name: VA Omaha Medical Center
resources.description: Main VA medical center for the Nebraska-Western Iowa Health Care System. Provides comprehensive healthcare services for veterans.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM. After hours nurse line: 1-800-451-5796
resources.eligibility: Veterans enrolled in VA healthcare. Call 833-983-0492 for enrollment information.

resources.name: VA Lincoln Clinic
resources.description: VA outpatient clinic providing primary care, mental health, and specialty services for veterans in the Lincoln area.
resources.eligibility: Veterans enrolled in VA healthcare.

resources.name: Grand Island VA Medical Center
resources.description: VA medical center providing comprehensive healthcare for veterans in central Nebraska.
resources.eligibility: Veterans enrolled in VA healthcare.

resources.name: Boys Town National Headquarters
resources.description: World-famous child-care facility providing residential care, treatment programs, and family services. About 400 boys and girls live in the Village receiving care for behavioral, emotional, and academic issues.
resources.hours: 24/7 residential care. Office: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
resources.eligibility: Youth with behavioral, emotional, or academic challenges. Contact for referral and intake process.

resources.name: Boys Town National Hotline
resources.description: 24/7 crisis hotline for kids and parents. Trained counselors handle calls for youth in crisis, including suicide prevention. Has prevented more than 2,700 active suicides in progress.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year
resources.eligibility: Any youth or parent in crisis. Free and confidential.

resources.name: Boys Town - South Omaha Services
resources.description: Boys Town community services office in South Omaha providing local family support and youth programs.
resources.eligibility: Youth and families in the South Omaha community.

resources.name: Project Harmony Child Advocacy Center
resources.description: One of the largest child advocacy centers in the country. Provides forensic interviews, medical exams, mental health services, and advocacy for children who have experienced abuse or neglect.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri. Call for specific hours. Missing Youth Hotline: 1-800-652-1999
resources.eligibility: Children who have experienced abuse, neglect, or other trauma.

resources.name: Youth Emergency Services (YES)
resources.description: Provides emergency shelter, transitional living, and support services for homeless and at-risk youth in the Omaha area. Over 50 years of service.
resources.hours: Drop-In Center: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM. 24/7 Safe Place Crisis Line: 402-578-7233
resources.eligibility: Youth experiencing homelessness or at risk in the Omaha area.

resources.name: Nebraska Children's Home Society (NCHS)
resources.description: Since 1893, provides education, adoption facilitation, foster care support, pregnancy services, and kinship navigation. Only agency in the US that does not charge adoption fees.
resources.eligibility: Families, children, and expectant parents across Nebraska. Eight locations statewide.

resources.name: Nebraska Children's Home Society - Children & Family Center
resources.description: NCHS satellite location providing family support services in North Omaha.
resources.eligibility: Families and children in need of support services.

resources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
resources.description: National suicide prevention and crisis lifeline providing 24/7 free and confidential support. Call or text 988. Nebraska has local crisis centers connected to this network.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
resources.eligibility: Anyone in emotional distress or suicidal crisis. Free and confidential.

resources.name: Nebraska 211
resources.description: Statewide information and referral helpline connecting people with health and human services. Call 2-1-1 or text your zip code to 898211.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Also text zip code to 898211. Toll-free: 866-813-1731. Email: ne211@uwmidlands.org
resources.eligibility: Anyone in Nebraska seeking help finding local services.

resources.name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
resources.description: 24/7 national hotline for domestic violence victims providing safety planning, shelter referrals, and support.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Text START to 88788.
resources.eligibility: Anyone experiencing domestic violence. Free and confidential.

resources.name: The Trevor Project
resources.description: Leading national organization providing crisis intervention and suicide prevention services to LGBTQ+ young people ages 13-24.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Also chat at thetrevorproject.org/get-help
resources.eligibility: LGBTQ+ young people ages 13-24. Free and confidential.

resources.name: Crisis Text Line
resources.description: Free 24/7 text-based crisis support. Text START to 741741 to connect with a trained crisis counselor.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
resources.eligibility: Anyone in crisis. Free and confidential.

resources.name: Trans Lifeline
resources.description: 24/7 hotline staffed by transgender people for transgender people. Provides crisis support and peer connection.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
resources.eligibility: Transgender individuals in crisis. Free and confidential.

resources.name: National Child Abuse Hotline
resources.description: 24/7 national hotline for reporting child abuse or seeking help and information about child welfare.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Call or text.
resources.eligibility: Anyone with concerns about child abuse or neglect.

resources.name: National Sexual Assault Hotline (RAINN)
resources.description: 24/7 national hotline connecting callers with local sexual assault service providers for crisis support and referrals.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Also online chat at rainn.org.
resources.eligibility: Anyone affected by sexual violence. Free and confidential.

resources.name: Disaster Distress Helpline
resources.description: 24/7 national helpline for emotional distress related to any natural or human-caused disaster.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Text TalkWithUs to 66746. Press 2 for Spanish.
resources.eligibility: Anyone experiencing emotional distress related to disasters. Free and confidential.

resources.name: All Nations Crisis Hotline
resources.description: Free text-based crisis service specifically for Native communities in Nebraska.
resources.eligibility: Native communities in Nebraska. Free.

resources.name: Veterans Crisis Line
resources.description: 24/7 crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families. Call 988 then press 1, or text 838255.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Text 838255. Chat at veteranscrisisline.net.
resources.eligibility: Veterans, active duty service members, National Guard, Reserve members, and their families/friends. Free and confidential.

resources.name: ACCESSNebraska - Economic Assistance
resources.description: Nebraska DHHS portal for applying for SNAP (food assistance), TANF, childcare subsidies, and other economic assistance programs.
resources.eligibility: Nebraska residents meeting income guidelines. Apply online at accessnebraska.ne.gov or via iServe Nebraska portal.

resources.name: ACCESSNebraska - Medicaid
resources.description: Nebraska DHHS Medicaid enrollment and information. Provides health coverage for eligible low-income individuals and families.
resources.eligibility: Nebraska residents meeting income and eligibility guidelines.

resources.name: Social Security Administration - Omaha
resources.description: Federal office handling Social Security benefits, SSI, SSDI, Medicare enrollment, and Social Security cards.
resources.hours: By appointment only. Call to schedule.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Appointment required.

resources.name: Social Security Administration - Lincoln
resources.description: Federal office handling Social Security benefits, SSI, SSDI, Medicare enrollment, and Social Security cards.
resources.hours: By appointment only. Call to schedule.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Appointment required.

resources.name: Social Security Administration - Grand Island
resources.description: Federal office handling Social Security benefits, SSI, SSDI, Medicare enrollment, and Social Security cards.
resources.hours: By appointment only. Call to schedule.
resources.eligibility: Open to all. Appointment required.

resources.name: Nebraska Department of Labor - Omaha American Job Center
resources.description: State job center providing employment services, job training, resume help, and unemployment resources through the Heartland Workforce Solutions partnership.
resources.eligibility: Open to all job seekers and employers in Nebraska.

resources.name: Nebraska Department of Labor - Lincoln American Job Center
resources.description: State job center providing employment services, job training, resume help, and unemployment resources.
resources.eligibility: Open to all job seekers and employers in Nebraska.

resources.name: Nebraska Department of Labor - Grand Island American Job Center
resources.description: State job center providing employment services, job training, and workforce development in central Nebraska.
resources.address: 203 East Stolley Park Road, Suite A
resources.eligibility: Open to all job seekers and employers in Nebraska.

resources.name: Nebraska Department of Labor - Beatrice American Job Center
resources.description: State job center providing employment and training services in southeast Nebraska.
resources.eligibility: Open to all job seekers and employers in Nebraska.

resources.name: Nebraska Vocational Rehabilitation
resources.description: State program helping people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Offices throughout Nebraska.
resources.eligibility: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that result in a barrier to employment.

resources.name: Nebraska Adult Education / GED Programs
resources.description: State-administered adult education programs providing GED preparation, English language learning, and basic skills throughout Nebraska. GED testing vouchers provided at no charge to enrolled students.
resources.hours: Program hours vary by location. Contact for local schedules.
resources.eligibility: Adults 16+ not enrolled in high school. Free programs available.

resources.name: VA Nebraska-Western Iowa Health Care System
resources.description: Comprehensive VA health care system serving veterans across Nebraska and western Iowa with medical centers in Omaha, Lincoln, and Grand Island plus community clinics.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM. After-hours nurse: 1-800-451-5796. Community clinics in Bellevue, Holdrege, Norfolk, North Platte, O'Neill.
resources.eligibility: Veterans. Call 833-983-0492 for enrollment. TTY: dial 711.

resources.name: MICAH House
resources.description: Emergency shelter serving families and women in the Omaha and Council Bluffs metro area.
resources.eligibility: Families and women in the Omaha/Council Bluffs metro area.

resources.name: SNAP/Food Resources Hotline (Nebraska)
resources.description: Statewide hotline helping Nebraska residents find food pantries and apply for SNAP benefits.
resources.eligibility: Nebraska residents seeking food assistance or SNAP benefits.

resources.name: VA Health Care Advice Nurse Line
resources.description: Toll-free nurse advice line for veterans needing medical guidance after hours, on weekends, and holidays.
resources.hours: Evenings after 4:00 PM, weekends, and holidays
resources.eligibility: Veterans enrolled in VA healthcare.
